comments: Next weeks session from Taurus and from the archives The Yardbirds.
TV plugs the BBC LP 'Metal Explosion' a collection of session tracks.
TV exposes the secret backwards message on side 2 of Pink Floyd's The Wall.

transcript.text: On 275 and 285, also in stereo on VHF, this is National Radio 1.
Oh hello there, good evening to you. This is Thomas Vance here, TV on the radio, and welcome once again to the Friday Rock Show.
This evening, the session debut of four ladies who are turning the tables on the male heavy rock contingent in the United Kingdom.
They're called Girl School and they're very good. Also, we have a repeat session by the band called Trespass.
now we've got classic cuts by the scorpions the eagles and the rolling stones among others and
new releases by gillan four tracks from his new albums hawkwind also peter hamill blue
oyster cult raven and holocaust and the return of the friday night connection the one of a set
three weeks ago by a gentleman in newport that we start off with acdc my favorite track of last
year the scorpions and loving you sunday morning another special for everybody at the cheslin hay
high school and they include all the people who go to wing gates in conningham lots of love from
sarah wilson who lives in essington in wolverhampton scorpions also for martin kilmer and everybody at
the turton high school martin lives in bromley cross in bolton john busby also known as rupe
wants me to say hello to all his mates at whitley bay and i've got to say hello to egg and cool cat
and hendy and sister mucus great name richie and stuart bowman in form three yours faithfully it
says here a group of mental cases say hello to the children of the great grave that is including
herbie and dozie mark and waldo and tommy ring my bell thompson and dave the raven moman kendo
and spiff and spanner and jim we live in didsbury in manchester and finally for esther and trout
and all the heavy metal fans who go to st nicholas's high school in northwich
before that you heard something from the new album called back in black by acdc the first
album that featured their new lead vocalist brian johnson the track was called shoot to
thrill i played that for all the other six who've now left west buckland school including mark and
pete and gas or gan i think it is nelly margaret and jazz and also for philip inman of hayward road
pile or possibly pill in avon and i like the way that ac dc have titled that album back in black
i think a tribute to bond and that's hawk win from their album live 1979 and a track called
motorways city and i played that for andrew stagg who lives in warwick and he wanted me to mention
everybody at binswood hall sixth form college which had just done i was really shocked to read
in the music press this week that simon king yet again here according to the music press has been
fired from hawkwind on account of the fact that they don't think he's a good enough drummer for
the band anymore to me he's been magnificent for years 10 22 on the friday rock show an intriguing
letter here from andy collie who lives in hillside at number five cargill road maybowl in ayrshire and
and I'll read this portion of the letter out to you.
Dear Tom, I need your help.
I don't know if you knew, and we're just keeping it a secret,
but there is a secret message on Pink Floyd's The Wall.
On side two, immediately before empty spaces,
there are words spoken, Roger's voice, I think,
which make no sense when played forward,
but when the disc is run backwards, the following is heard.
Congratulations, you've just discovered the secret message.
Send your answer to Old Pink, care of the funny farm.
And Andy writes, Chelford here,
Well, with a bit of jiggery-pokery and some fine engineering by Mr. Tony Wilson and our engineer Paul tonight,
we've sort of done that same trick in the studio, but also done it a little bit better because of our equalizers and things like this.
So, if you run your record backwards, a little bit before empty spaces, this is what you'll hear.
Got my directions mixed up a bit there.
First you heard it backwards, and then you heard it forwards, whichever way you want to look at it.
But we figure out they actually say on the album something like,
Hello, Walter. Congratulations, you've just discovered the secret message.
Send your answer to Old Pink, care of the funny farm in Chalfont.
And then there's a voice that says, Roger, Caroline's on the phone.
We're going to see Pink Floyd next week, as indeed are thousands of other people.
And if I can collar Mr. Waters, I'll ask him what it's all about.
Well spotted, Andy Colley. And here now is the first of four by Girl School.
...stressless and a track called Visionary, and now here's some really nice news.
And the good news is that that track is going to be included on a new compilation album
which is coming out during the month of September on BBC Records, nonetheless.
Total quality.
Now, the album's going to be called Metal Explosion,
and it's going to be built around various tracks that have been recorded
specially for Friday Rock Show Sessions.
Hard to say, then.
That's coming out in September.
It's going to include a load of good music, and I'll tell you more about it.
as the month of August progresses up to the release date.
Okay, the album's going to be called Metal Explosion.
When it gets into the shops, buy it because we need the money.
And on top of that, the music's good.
Prior to that, you heard Girl School on the 1st of 4
from their debut session here on the BBC.
The track was called Nothing to Lose.
Tomorrow, on the Saturday Rock Show,
otherwise known as Rock on Saturday,
I'm going to be talking to Ian Gillan,
and he's got a new album out which is called Glory Road.
This is just one of the tracks.
A couple of tracks from the new album
that's out by the man and the band.
The album's called Glory Road.
That track, if you believe me,
first saw the light of broadcasting on this program
when he specially recorded it for us some time ago.
And before that, you heard a track called Are You Sure?
That also is in Glory Road.
The first 15,000 people to buy that album
will find that they'll get an extra album as well,
which is called For Gillan Fans Only.
And I'm going to be chatting about that to Mr. Gillan
on Rock on Saturday tomorrow afternoon between 4 and 7.30.
Ian Gillan is one of the most articulate men in rock and roll, I believe,
and a man that really is in touch with his fans.
And I'd like to thank him very much on your behalf,
because I know you enjoyed it for sitting in on this programme a fortnight ago.
And of course, the band Gillan are going to be guesting
on the Friday evening of the Reading Festival this year.
And the Friday Rock Show is going to be there throughout the three days,
recording, well, about 12 of the acts who are going to hit the stage.
13 minutes before 11, let's go back to Girl School.
Can't make things any plainer
Let's get in the way
is that quite if not now then certainly sooner or later we've got a problem with communication
look i scrabble with my hands well i try to get some headroom from the elevation but you just don't
understand so much better than anything with precision album that's come out on s type records
The album's called A Black Box.
In the track you heard there was entitled Losing Faith in Words.
And I played it because Peter Hamill wrote me a personal letter,
for which I'm very thankful.
He just said, Dear Tom, here's A Black Box.
I believe it contains some of the best work I've ever done,
either solo or with Van de Graaff.
It is resolutely of the 80s.
See what you think.
And I think it is.
Prior to that, you heard Girl School,
and the second of four we're playing tonight in the Friday Rock Show.
From our session by then, the track was Breakdown,
and it featured lead guitarist Kelly Johnson on the lead vocals.
And that will make Tommy Muir of the Orkneys more than happy
because he's in love with all four of them.
Band here by the name of Holocaust.
A blue oyster cult and a track from their new album which is out on CBS.
The album is called Cultosaurus Erectus
and the track you heard there was called Monsters.
They always come up with something different, that band.
They're very sophisticated and they really know what they're doing.
And I'm looking forward to one of these days seeing them again.
I haven't seen them, oh, it must be in about four years.
Prior to that you heard something from a 12-incher
by a band called Holocaust.
The track you heard was Heavy Metal Mania.
Don't know too much about the band.
Got a short press release in front of me
that was generated up there in Edinburgh.
It just says that the band are all 17 years of age
and they're the first act on Phoenix Record and Filmworks.
So if you want to find it, it's a 12-incher
and it's on the Phoenix label.
Back to Trespass.
That was Trespass and the studio version
of what was their first single.
The track was called One of These Days
and that was recorded in April of this year.
I hear now that they've got a new lead singer
by the name of Rob Eklund
and there are going to be some London gigs
at the end of August,
but no dates have been fixed as yet.
And that's all the trustless information
I've got for you tonight.
Nine minutes after 11,
it's time for the Friday Night Connection.
The return of this free,
gratis and for nothing,
titillating, tantalising part of the week.
And tonight we're going to play
the Friday Night Connection
that was put together by our last winner,
who is Mr Mark Studley,
who lives at 63 Vancouver Drive, Newport and Gwent
who should be a £10 record token richer now.
Three pieces of music with a definite theme
and a definite connection.
Starting off with this.
There you have the return of the Friday Night Connection.
Now, you heard three pieces of music
and there is most definitely a connection involved
in those three slices of success
which were put together by Mark Stutley
who lives in Newport and Gwent.
If you think you know what the connection is
then just jot it down on a postcard
and send it to this address.
The Friday Night Connection,
The Friday Rock Show,
BBC Radio 1,
London,
W1A4WW.
But don't forget your side of the deal as well.
We need from you three bits of music
which have a definite connection or theme.
And if you are the person
that is mentioned around about this time next week,
we'll have a £10 record token to play with.
back to the sixth form and girl school good band girl school and demolition which you'll find on
their album of the same name that that of course was our studio version because we're featuring

tomorrow night in folkestone they'll be at the lees cliff hall and then they're in hull and

and a track called outlaw man
All my friends are straight

Don't try to understand

Great piece of recording.

from our session by then.

not just a few, wanted to hear Trespass again
on the Friday Rock Show, including Anna Murray
of Dundee in Scotland, who wrote on our card
I wrote before and you ignored me.
But I'm not now, Alan. I'm just going to say hello to all her mates that went to see Girls' School in concert in Dundee on July the 9th.
And that's Grace and Carmel and Andy Barrett and Ian Hood and Leslie Carroll.

and it was called Unchain Your Brain.

I'm going to be talking to Ian Gillan tomorrow

on Radio 1 between 4 and 7.30.
And I'm going to ask him all about this
For Gillan Fans Only LP.
It has a whole selection of oddments
that have been recorded over the years
and haven't been released in this country before.
Okay, this is the Friday Rock Show.
It's 14 minutes to midnight.
25 Years of Rock rolls round this week to the year 1961.
John F. Kennedy becomes President of the United States
and has to deal with Russian rockets in Cuba.
Rudolf Nureyev defects from Russia
as the Berlin Wall begins to rise.
Chubby Checker does the twist,
Dave Brubeck takes five,
and Barry Mann asks,
Who put the bomb?
1961, a most dramatic year.
That's 25 Years of Rock, Radio 1, Sunday, 7 o'clock.
Dancing with Mr. D from the album Goat's Head Soup.
It came out in 1973. I didn't know it was that long ago.
I had to look on the label to find out exactly when.
That, of course, was the Rolling Stones.
And that was for Angus and Justine and everybody that goes to or has recently retired from Blundell School in Tiverton.
And before that, you heard The Last of Four Tonight by Girl School from our session by them.
It was the track called Take It All Away.
Kelly Johnson on lead guitar and vocals,
Kim McAleef on guitar and vocals,
Denny's Dufour on the drums,
and Di Williams on the bass.
Somebody made a nasty comment today on the radio,
can't remember who it was, thought it was a bit cruel.

looks something like a Romanian athlete.
Bit cruel.
If you get the chance to go and see the band, do so,
because they can play their instruments marvellously.
I've just heard that this is going to come out

And that was Trespass, and that was called Live It Up.
And that's it for this edition of the Friday Rock Show.
A quick reminder that on Rock on Saturday tomorrow,

Gillan, I'll be talking to him, and we'll have Bow Wow Wow.
That's 4 to 7.30 tomorrow.
And on the Friday Rock Show next week,
a repeat of our session by Taurus and Out of the Archives
will have the Yardbirds.
Many thanks to our engineer tonight, who has been Paul.
And I tell you, the last four and a half hours of Radio 1
have been brought to you by that eminent producer, Tony Wilson,
who's still awake, aren't you, Tony?
Yes, can I go to bed now, please?
and goodnight, Wandsworth.
